channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lambrini Girls cancel Coachella performance after lead singer Phoebe Lunny suffers severe neck and brain injury
item.description: British punk band Lambrini Girls announced that they would not perform at Coachella 2026 after their lead vocalist, Phoebe Lunny, sustained a fractured neck and an acute brain injury while in Australi...

item.title: Camiguin Turns to Solar Energy Amid High Power Costs and Frequent Brownouts
item.description: Camiguin province in the Philippines has increasingly adopted solar energy to address high electricity costs and frequent brownouts. Residents like Graham Pilapil invested in household solar systems, ...

item.title: Declining Vaccination Rates Threaten Herd Immunity in Allegheny County Schools
item.description: Vaccination coverage in Allegheny County, Pennsylvania, is showing concerning trends, especially among kindergartners. Data from the 2023-24 school year reveals that one in three kindergarten classroo...

item.title: New Brunswick Police Association Challenges Chief’s Handling of Harassment Complaints
item.description: The New Brunswick Police Association has publicly refuted claims made by Saint John Police Chief Robert Bruce that workplace harassment complaints against him were union-driven tactics. Chief Bruce, w...

item.title: DA Congress Urged to Focus on Unity and Collaboration
item.description: The upcoming Democratic Alliance (DA) congress represents a pivotal moment for the party, as well as for the broader political landscape in South Africa. In a reader letter, Thulani Dasa from Khayelit...

item.title: Memorial Unveiled for Three Soldiers Killed in Iraq
item.description: A permanent memorial has been unveiled in Tamworth, Staffordshire, to honour three soldiers who tragically lost their lives in Iraq more than 20 years ago. Private Leon Spicer, Private Phillip Hewett,...

item.title: Health Challenges and Adaptations for Artemis Astronauts During Short-Term Space Missions
item.description: The article explores how a 10-day journey in space, such as one planned for NASA's Artemis missions, could impact the physical and mental well-being of astronauts. Even relatively short spaceflights e...

item.title: Scientists Confirm 1958 Vitamin B1 Theory by Stabilizing Carbene in Water
item.description: A research team at the University of California, Riverside, has successfully stabilized a highly reactive molecule in water, confirming a 67-year-old theory originally proposed by chemist Ronald Bresl...

item.title: Police investigate complaint regarding Reform UK's energy bills giveaway
item.description: Authorities are reviewing a complaint concerning a competition organised by Reform UK that offered free energy bills to participants. The police have been called in to examine whether the competition ...

item.title: Over 200 People Arrested in London Protest Against Palestine Action Ban
item.description: On Saturday, London’s Metropolitan Police detained 212 protesters in Trafalgar Square who were demonstrating against the UK government’s ban on the Palestine Action group. This protest was the first p...

item.title: Dispute Over EVM Strongroom Protocol in Kerala Resolved
item.description: A tense situation arose in Kottayam, Kerala, on Thursday evening when a Central Observer tried to change the existing protocol for securing electronic voting machines (EVMs) in the strongroom at Basel...

item.title: Southport Inquiry Examines Causes and Failures Behind Knife Attack
item.description: The Southport Inquiry was established to investigate the tragic knife attack at a Taylor Swift-themed dance workshop in Southport on 29 July 2024, which claimed the lives of three young girls, Alice d...

item.title: Canberra property market sees mixed auction outcomes after Easter
item.description: The Canberra property market experienced a mix of auction results over the post-Easter period, with several standout sales amid generally patchy outcomes. Notable sales included a grand five-bedroom h...

item.title: Artemis II Crew Shares Experiences After Completing 10-Day Lunar Flyby
item.description: After completing a 10-day mission that took them around the far side of the moon, NASA's Artemis II astronauts returned to Earth to a warm welcome in Houston. Commander Reid Wiseman described the miss...

item.title: Bahati and Diana Marua Discuss Responsible Pocket Money Use with Son Morgan
item.description: Veteran musician Bahati and his wife Diana Marua recently sat down with their son, Morgan, to talk about how he has been using his pocket money. The discussion comes after Morgan received over KSh 10,...

item.title: Ongoing Developments in Middle East Conflict as of April 12, 2026
item.description: As of April 12, 2026, tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, with ongoing clashes between US and Iranian forces and other regional actors. The Strait of Hormuz remains a critical focal poin...

item.title: DA Promises Open Elections Amidst Leadership Transition
item.description: Outgoing DA Federal Council chairperson Helen Zille has assured delegates that upcoming elections for new party leadership will be conducted with openness and fairness. Addressing the DA’s federal con...

item.title: Rep. Robert Garcia Responds to Melania Trump’s Epstein Statement and Urges Further Cooperation

item.title: Health risks of lead in silver three-legged pots highlighted on World Health Day
item.description: During World Health Day, South African health officials, researchers, and experts raised concerns about lead exposure from cookware produced in the informal sector, including silver three-legged pots....

item.title: Nova Scotian Music Industry Veteran Joe Wood Recognized by National Music Centre
